title: virrun configuration
description: The repo-root virrun.config.json: a checked-in, reviewable selection of which backend a sandboxed command runs through (adoption level 3). The `virrun -- <cmd>` prefix is the switch for whether a command is sandboxed; this config only chooses how. Every field is optional and falls back to a safe default, so a minimal {} — or an absent file — is valid and the backend defaults to auto (native today).

$schema.description: Path or URL to this JSON schema, for editor autocompletion and validation.

backend.description: The backend a sandboxed command runs through. Defaults to `auto` (resolves to native today). Mirror of the BackendType enum in src/models/virrun/BackendType.ts.
enumDescriptions: Pick the best backend the host supports — native today, until an isolating backend beats the gates.
Run the command directly on the host with no virtualization.
Run inside a bubblewrap RAM-overlay (Linux, or Windows via WSL2): reads fall through to source, writes vanish in tmpfs. Ephemeral — only safe for read-only verification commands.
Run recognised pure-JS node invocations in-process; falls back to native for everything else. Opt-in, no isolation.

environment.description: The framework whose source-derived artifacts the sandbox regenerates into a source-keyed prepare layer (e.g. Nuxt's .nuxt), so type-aware tooling reads a platform-correct, fresh copy instead of the host's. Defaults to `none`. Mirror of the Environment enum in src/models/virrun/Environment.ts.
enumDescriptions: No framework prepare step; the warm cache is deps-only and any source-derived postinstall artifact is pruned.
A Nuxt app: capture `nuxt prepare` output (.nuxt) into a source-keyed prepare layer, refreshed only when source changes.
